description: Identity-verified permission system. Every message resolves to a Slack ID or motionapp.com email; admins can edit org-level paths and standing instructions, team members get their own home base and write only there. Includes prompt-injection defenses and auto-provisioning of unknown verified IDs.

requires.runtime: any Runneth sandbox; jq must be installed

post-install.name: Run the five-phase deployment
post-install.description: Invokes the skill's full deployment: pre-flight scan, summary + confirmation, file writes, post-deploy verification, post-deploy setup checklist.

post-install.name: Introduce the new capability
post-install.description: Read post-install-intro.md from this use case folder. After all prior install and post-install steps complete, surface the file's sections to the user in chat as the closing message of the install turn: '## What just opened up' as prose, '## Try this now' as a numbered prompt list, '## Compounds with' only when present and at least one named use case is installed in the sandbox. Resolve any {{TOKEN}} placeholders using the same customize map applied during install.

changelog.notes: Two changes: (1) renamed workspace-map.json to organization-map.json (clearer: it is the organization-wide identity registry, not a workspace-scoped artifact); env override variable renamed RUNNETH_WORKSPACE_MAP to RUNNETH_ORG_MAP. (2) motion-whoami.sh is now Neon-only: queries the Neon agent_conversation table via a new motion-whoami-neon.py helper (invoked through secret run --env DATABASE_URL=NEON_DATABASE_URL) for authoritative, zero-lag identity resolution. No SQLite fallback — on Neon failure the script exits non-zero and the permissions layer treats the user as unknown (no writes) per the §2.Unknown rule. Accepts CONVERSATION_ID from env in addition to cwd-basename. Fixes the failure mode where brand-new conversations could not be resolved because the live conversations.db is a 0-byte placeholder until the backup cycle. Migration of existing orgs is deferred. Refs: PDEC-7817.
Added post-install intro. After install completes, Runneth surfaces a short 'what just opened up + try this now' message sourced from post-install-intro.md.
Initial install-config. Replaces ad-hoc skill-invoke install with the standard protocol.
